title: pyrus collivaga
intro: Pyrus collivaga, also known as the wild pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Asia. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus collivaga are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture for medicinal use, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects.
